matter.title: Estate of Grace C. Roe: Will Contest (Roe v. Roe)
matter.summary: Rosa T. Roe, a disinherited adult child of Grace C. Roe, brings a formal testacy proceeding in Sagadahoc County Probate Court to deny probate of a will dated 2024-01-19 that left nearly the entire estate to a late-life romantic companion, alleging the testator lacked testamentary capacity, was subject to undue influence, and that the will was not properly executed.

defendant.role: Proponent (the favored beneficiary / named personal representative)

fact_pattern.summary: Rosa T. Roe, a disinherited adult child of Grace C. Roe, brings a formal testacy proceeding in Sagadahoc County Probate Court to deny probate of a will dated 2024-01-19 that left nearly the entire estate to a late-life romantic companion, alleging the testator lacked testamentary capacity, was subject to undue influence, and that the will was not properly executed.
fact_pattern.narrative:
Grace C. Roe died on 2025-09-20, leaving an estate worth roughly $2756024. Omar D. Roe has offered for probate a will dated 2024-01-19 and seeks appointment as personal representative. That will leaves nearly the entire estate to a late-life romantic companion, while Rosa T. Roe and Felix K. Loe, the decedent's other children, take little or nothing.

The contested will was made late in the decedent's life, at a time when the decedent was experiencing cognitive decline following a stroke. Rosa alleges that the favored beneficiary cultivated a relationship of trust and dependence, isolated the decedent from family, and arranged the new will to the beneficiary's own advantage. Rosa further alleges the will was not validly executed -- the witnesses did not see the testator sign -- so it does not satisfy the requirements of 18-C M.R.S. § 2-502.

Rosa therefore petitions for formal testacy under 18-C M.R.S. § 3-401 et seq., asking the court to deny probate of the 2024-01-19 will for lack of testamentary capacity, undue influence, and improper execution. In a contested formal proceeding the proponent must prove due execution, while the contestants bear the burden on incapacity and undue influence, 18-C M.R.S. § 3-407. Rosa asks the court to admit the decedent's earlier will instead, or, if none controls, to distribute the estate by intestacy.
undisputed_facts: Grace C. Roe died on 2025-09-20 domiciled in Sagadahoc County, Maine.
Omar D. Roe has offered a will dated 2024-01-19 for formal probate and seeks appointment as personal representative.
Rosa T. Roe is an adult child and heir of the decedent with standing to contest the will.
disputed_facts: Whether the decedent had testamentary capacity when the 2024-01-19 will was signed, 18-C M.R.S. § 2-501.
Whether the contested will was the product of undue influence by the favored beneficiary.
Whether the will was executed in conformity with 18-C M.R.S. § 2-502 and, if applicable, whether an earlier will controls under 18-C M.R.S. § 2-507.

timeline.event: The decedent executed an earlier estate plan said to provide for the children more equally.
The decedent executed the contested will favoring a late-life romantic companion.
Petition for formal testacy and to contest the will prepared for filing in Bath Probate Court.
Grace C. Roe died, and the contested will was offered for probate.

third_parties.name: Pine Tree Community Bank
third_parties.role: Bank or financial advisor for the estate accounts
third_parties.relevance: Holds or managed the decedent's accounts and estate assets whose records show the favored beneficiary's involvement.

third_parties.name: Wesley B. Fairbanks
third_parties.role: Second attesting witness to the contested will
third_parties.relevance: The other attesting witness, relevant to whether the execution requirements of 18-C M.R.S. § 2-502 were satisfied.

third_parties.name: Theodore S. Larkspur
third_parties.role: Attesting witness to the contested will
third_parties.relevance: Signed the will as an attesting witness and can speak to whether the testator signed in the witnesses' presence.

third_parties.name: Wesley B. Poe
third_parties.relevance: Treated the decedent around the time the contested will was signed and can speak to the decedent's cognition and testamentary capacity.

exchanges.question: What outcome are you hoping for in contesting the will?
exchanges.answer: I want the court to reject the last will. It does not reflect what my parent actually wanted. If there is an earlier will, that one should stand; otherwise the estate should pass the normal way among us children.

exchanges.question: What does the contested will say, and how is it different from before?
exchanges.answer: The will from 2024-01-19 gives nearly the entire estate to a late-life romantic companion. That is a complete reversal from how my parent always said things would be divided among the family.

exchanges.question: What was your parent's condition when that will was signed?
exchanges.answer: Around that time my parent was dealing with cognitive decline following a stroke. There were good days and bad days, but I do not believe my parent understood what that document did or who it cut out.

exchanges.question: What makes you think there was undue influence?
exchanges.answer: The beneficiary moved in close, took over the finances and the calls, and kept the rest of us at a distance. By the end we could barely reach our own parent without going through them.

exchanges.question: Do you have concerns about how the will was signed?
exchanges.answer: Yes. From what we have learned, the witnesses did not see the testator sign. It was not done the careful way you would expect for something this important.

exchanges.question: How do you feel about the cost and the family strain of a contest?
exchanges.answer: It is painful to fight with family, and I know it is not cheap. I am willing to push this if the facts hold up, but I would rather resolve it sensibly than turn it into a war.

goals: Deny probate of the 2024-01-19 will for lack of testamentary capacity, undue influence, and improper execution.
Have the decedent's prior will admitted, or, if none controls, have the estate distributed by intestacy.
Expose the favored beneficiary's undue influence and isolation of the decedent and ensure a fair distribution among the heirs.
priorities: Building a clear record on capacity at the time of signing and on the execution defects.
Documenting the confidential relationship and the isolation of the decedent.
Protecting and accounting for the estate's assets during the contest.
desired_outcomes: An order denying probate of the contested will and admitting the prior will or directing intestate distribution.
A fair share of the estate restored to the disinherited children.
non_starters: Any resolution that lets the contested will stand on its current terms.

constraints.budget: Measured; prefers a negotiated family resolution but will try the contest if the evidence supports it.
constraints.timeline: Wants the will contest resolved before the estate is distributed under the contested will.

issues.issue: Due execution of the contested will
sub_questions: Was the will signed by the testator and attested by at least two competent witnesses?
Did the witnesses witness the signing or the testator's acknowledgment as the statute requires?
issues.client_position: The will was not executed in conformity with the statute, so it cannot be admitted to probate.

issues.issue: Lack of testamentary capacity at the time the will was signed
sub_questions: Did the testator understand the nature of the act, the property, and the natural objects of the testator's bounty?
Does the medical and cognitive evidence around the signing show a lack of capacity?
issues.client_position: The testator's cognitive condition left Rosa's parent without the capacity required to make a valid will.

issues.issue: Allocation of the burdens of proof in a contested formal testacy proceeding
sub_questions: Has the proponent carried the burden of proving due execution?
Have the contestants carried the burden on incapacity, undue influence, fraud, duress, mistake, or revocation?
issues.client_position: The proponent cannot prove due execution, and the contestants can meet their burden on incapacity and undue influence.

issues.issue: Order of formal probate and supervision of the administration
sub_questions: Should the court adjudicate testacy in a formal proceeding?
Should administration be supervised to protect the estate during the contest?
issues.client_position: The court should resolve testacy formally and supervise the administration to safeguard the estate.

issues.issue: Whether a prior will controls or the estate passes by intestacy
sub_questions: Was the prior will validly executed and never revoked?
If the contested will is denied probate, does the prior will or intestacy govern?
issues.client_position: If the contested will fails, the decedent's earlier will should be admitted, or the estate should pass by intestacy.

issues.issue: Effect of a self-proved will and the absence of a self-proving affidavit
sub_questions: Was the will accompanied by a valid self-proving affidavit?
If not, must the proponent prove execution through the attesting witnesses?
issues.client_position: The will is not self-proved, so the proponent must prove its execution through live testimony that the irregularities undermine.

expert_opinions.topic: Testamentary capacity at the time the will was signed
expert_opinions.opinion: At the time of execution, the testator's cognitive condition impaired the capacity to understand the nature of the act, the extent of the property, and the natural objects of the testator's bounty.
expert_opinions.basis: Review of the contemporaneous medical and cognitive records and the surrounding circumstances.

expert_opinions.topic: Customary practice for drafting and executing a will
expert_opinions.opinion: The drafting and execution of the will conformed to the customary standard of care for the circumstances.
expert_opinions.basis: The drafting file, the execution ceremony, and prevailing estate-planning practice.

evidence.title: The decedent's prior will
evidence.description: The earlier will the contestant says should control if the contested will is denied probate.

evidence.title: The drafting attorney's file and notes
evidence.description: The drafting file, intake notes, and execution records for the contested will.

evidence.title: The decedent's financial records showing the beneficiary's involvement
evidence.description: Account and asset records reflecting the favored beneficiary's access to and control of the decedent's finances.

evidence.title: The decedent's medical and cognitive records around the signing
evidence.description: Records and evaluations bearing on the decedent's capacity around the time the contested will was executed.

amounts.label: Estimated cost of the capacity and document experts
amounts.basis: Retained capacity and forensic document examiners.

amounts.label: Contestant's intestate or prior-will share
amounts.basis: The share the contestant would take under a prior will or by intestacy.

amounts.label: Value of the share diverted to the favored beneficiary
amounts.basis: Portion of the estate the contested will leaves to the favored beneficiary.

elements: The testator executed the contested will
While lacking the capacity to understand the nature of the act, the property, and the natural objects of the testator's bounty
Due to the testator's cognitive condition at the time of signing
Rendering the will invalid
causes_of_action.relief_sought: An order denying probate of the contested will for lack of testamentary capacity

elements: A confidential relationship between the testator and the favored beneficiary
Susceptibility of the testator to influence
Opportunity and disposition of the beneficiary to exert influence
A will that reflects the influence rather than the testator's free will
causes_of_action.relief_sought: An order denying probate of the contested will as the product of undue influence

elements: The contested will was not signed by the testator and attested by at least two competent witnesses as required
The witnesses did not witness the signing or the testator's acknowledgment as the statute requires
The instrument therefore does not meet the statutory execution requirements
Rendering the will ineligible for probate
causes_of_action.relief_sought: An order denying probate of the contested will for want of due execution

causes_of_action.claim: Fraud or mistake in the will's execution
elements: A material misrepresentation to or mistake by the testator about the instrument
On which the testator relied in executing the will
That caused the will to differ from the testator's true intent
Rendering the will invalid
causes_of_action.relief_sought: An order denying probate of the contested will procured by fraud or mistake

causes_of_action.claim: Admission of the prior will and adjudication of testacy
elements: The decedent executed a valid prior will
That was never revoked under 18-C M.R.S. § 2-507
And remains the decedent's effective testamentary instrument
Entitling it to formal probate if the contested will is denied
causes_of_action.relief_sought: An order admitting the prior will to formal probate, or, if none controls, directing intestate distribution

affirmative_defenses.defense: The will is self-proved and validly executed
affirmative_defenses.basis: The will was duly executed and is self-proved, establishing its validity. 18-C M.R.S. § 2-502; 18-C M.R.S. § 2-504.

affirmative_defenses.defense: The testator had capacity and acted freely
affirmative_defenses.basis: The testator possessed testamentary capacity and executed the will of the testator's own free will. 18-C M.R.S. § 2-501.

affirmative_defenses.defense: The contest is barred by limitations
affirmative_defenses.basis: The contest was not commenced within the period allowed to challenge a formal testacy order.

counterclaims.claim: Petition to admit the contested will to formal probate
counterclaims.relief: Formal probate of the will dated 2024-01-19 and appointment of the proponent as personal representative.

discovery.description: Requests for the drafting file and signed medical authorizations for the decedent's records.
Subpoena to the bank for the decedent's account and estate records.
Depositions of the proponent, the drafting attorney, the attesting witnesses, and the treating physician.

motions.title: Motion for partial summary judgment on due execution
motions.relief: A ruling that the contested will was not duly executed under 18-C M.R.S. § 2-502.

motions.title: Motion to appoint a temporary special administrator
motions.relief: Appointment of a special administrator to protect the estate pending resolution of the contest.

motions.title: Motion to compel the decedent's medical records
motions.relief: An order compelling production of the decedent's medical and cognitive records.

motions.title: Motion for formal testacy and to supervise administration
motions.relief: An order adjudicating testacy in a formal proceeding and supervising the administration of the estate.

docket.entry: Petition for formal testacy and to contest the will filed.
Proponent's response and petition to admit the contested will filed.
Order appointing a temporary special administrator entered.
Order compelling the decedent's medical records entered.
Capacity expert's report served; discovery continues.
Hearing held on the motion regarding due execution.

facts.execution_irregularity: the witnesses did not see the testator sign
